summary: Found one candidate P1: the new final hash-bound human decision phase and final validation order appear inconsistent with the evaluator, so the documented workflow may not have a coherent accepted_merge_ready path after publication evidence is known.

findings.title: Final hash-bound acceptance phase is ordered after validation but is not accepted by the evaluator
evidence: workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml:272-289 defines final_readiness_gate immediately after readiness_report, then final_human_merge_decision after that gate. There is no later report regeneration or validation gate in the changed phase order.

scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py:442-449 validates accepted merge decisions only when the human decision item has phase_id == "human_merge_decision" and report_hash equal to sha256_file(report_path); the new final_human_merge_decision phase is not recognized.

scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py:1486-1491 validates the report by comparing declared status with the evaluator state. A report declaring accepted_merge_ready before the final hash-bound decision exists should fail; a report validated as awaiting_human_decision before that decision is not revalidated as accepted by the workflow.
docs/pr-merge-readiness.md:39-42 documents this order as produce final report, validate it, then record or refresh the final human merge decision with the exact report_hash.
findings.why_it_matters: Following the new workflow literally leaves the acceptance path ambiguous or broken. Either the pre-decision final gate cannot validate an accepted report because the hash-bound decision is not present yet, or it validates a non-accepted report and then records the final human decision without a subsequent authoritative acceptance validation. In addition, a decision recorded under the new final_human_merge_decision phase would not satisfy the current evaluator predicate.
findings.recommendation: Make the executable order and evaluator agree. One viable shape is: produce the final report artifact used for hashing, record the final hash-bound human acceptance using a phase_id the evaluator accepts, then run the final readiness validation that can compute accepted_merge_ready. Alternatively, update the evaluator to explicitly accept the final phase and add a post-final-decision report/regate step. Add regression coverage for the final_human_merge_decision phase_id and the full final report -> hash-bound decision -> accepted validation sequence.
findings.acceptance_impact: Accepted merge readiness may be unreachable or may be claimed without fresh post-decision validation after publication evidence and the final report hash are known.
